expectations.text: The response mentions the Desktop app's built-in browser or Chrome DevTools integration for testing web servers
expectations.evidence: 'built-in live app preview feature', 'embedded browser inside the Desktop app'. Also covers Chrome integration as a separate option.

expectations.text: The response explains how Claude can automatically start and interact with a web server
expectations.evidence: 'Claude starts your dev server automatically', auto-verify with screenshots/DOM inspection/clicking/form filling. Includes .claude/launch.json config.

expectations.text: The response mentions how to set up similar functionality in the CLI (e.g. via MCP or chrome-devtools)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'What About Chrome Integration?' section: 'Install the Claude in Chrome extension', 'Launch Claude Code with --chrome or run /chrome in a session'.
